title: Review Findings Artifact
description: Typed artifact produced by an adapter that declares the review_findings capability. Transported via adapter envelope → output_parse rule walker (in adapter_invoker._invocation_from_envelope) → InvocationResult.extracted_outputs['review_findings'] → Executor.run_step(driver_managed=True) → artifacts.write_artifact() → workflow-run.step_record.output_ref. The envelope itself is NOT schema-validated against this shape; this schema validates the EXTRACTED payload, which the rule walker produces after navigating the output_parse rule's json_path into the adapter's envelope body. Consumers: FAZ-B PR-B6 review_ai_flow runtime + PR-B7 governed_review benchmark harness. See docs/BENCHMARK-SUITE.md §2 for the full transport chain and objective scoring.

schema_version.description: Artifact schema version. Locked to '1' for the v1 contract; breaking changes would ship as review-findings.schema.v2.json.

findings.description: List of review findings. Empty array is legal (meaning: 'reviewed with no issues'); this is distinct from the adapter not returning a review-findings payload at all (which would fail extraction). The benchmark harness scores empty findings as a successful review run with no actionable issues.

summary.description: Human-readable one-line summary of the review. Subject to evidence redaction rules when copied into audit stream.

score.description: Optional. Reviewer self-confidence or coverage score, caller-defined semantics in [0.0, 1.0]. The governed_review benchmark harness may consume this as a threshold gate (e.g., 'reject run if score < 0.5'); absence is not a failure signal by itself.

file.description: Workspace-relative path the finding applies to. Optional — findings may be file-agnostic (e.g., 'test coverage < threshold across the module').

line.description: 1-indexed line number inside 'file'. Optional; only meaningful if 'file' is also set.

severity.description: Closed enum. 'error': blocking issue (must-fix). 'warning': should-fix concern. 'info': context or acknowledgement. 'note': inline annotation without verdict. 'critical' is deliberately NOT included (see CNS-20260416-028v2 iter-2 W7): adding a value that does not trigger a distinct policy/gate behaviour only inflates taxonomy; operators can filter by severity= ['error'] for the same effect.

message.description: Human-readable finding body. Subject to evidence redaction rules when copied into the audit stream.

suggestion.description: Optional. Proposed remediation or rewrite sketch. Not a diff — for a diff, use the adapter's write_diff capability instead.
